So, Ana, tell me about your first block. What are you proud of, and what was hard?
I have learned a lot. My milestone was week five, when I helped deploy a difficult feature on time.
That is great. And your daily workflow? How do you decide what to test first?
Every morning I check the backlog and I prioritize the bugs by severity. Then I look for the root cause.
And the hard part? You said something was difficult.
My biggest challenge was a blocker in week three. The environment broke, but I raised it and I followed up until it was fixed.
Well done. One tip for the next block: when a requirement is not clear, clarify it early, and think about the edge cases.
Thank you, Marcus. I will clarify early and check the edge cases. I am ready for Block 2.
Every morning I check the backlog and prioritize by severity.
My biggest challenge was a blocker, but I followed up.
When a requirement is not clear, I clarify it first.

Hi Ana, this is Marcus. I want to leave a quick note after our review call, so you have it in writing. First, congratulations on a strong first block. Your milestone in week five was excellent, and the way you found the root cause of that intermittent bug really helped the team. I also liked how you handled the blocker in week three; you did not stay silent, you raised it and you followed up. For the next block, my advice is simple. When a requirement is not clear, clarify it before you start, and think about the edge cases early. That will save you time. Keep up the good work, and let us talk again in two weeks.

Hi Ana, it is Priya, your team lead. I heard your review went well, and I am not surprised. Here is a short recap of what you have built in Block 1. You have learned the words of QA, from severity to root cause. You can give a clear stand-up update, you can read a bug report for the detail, and you can follow a colleague with a different accent. Most important, you have learned to speak up: to prioritize, to clarify, and to follow up. In Block 2, we go further. We will talk about requirements, passive voice for reporting problems, and conditionals for cause and effect. But the base is here, and it is strong. Well done, Ana.

First, Ana prepared a short summary before her review meeting with Marcus. Then she talked about her milestone in week five, when she helped deploy a difficult feature. Next, she explained her workflow and how she prioritizes bugs by severity. After that, she described her biggest challenge, a blocker in week three, and how she followed up until it was fixed. Finally, Marcus advised her to clarify unclear requirements and think about the edge cases early.
